page: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is a memory-optimized virtual server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features an AMD EPYC Genoa processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, configured with 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 200 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing placing the instance in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores falling into the average category. Memory performance is a notable strength, as evidenced by a strong PassMark memory score and strong large-block write memory bandwidth. Conversely, the instance exhibits weaknesses in multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving throughput, which all scored in the bottom 25% tier.

The primary tradeoff of this instance lies in its shared CPU allocation, which limits multi-threaded scaling and database throughput despite the high-capacity AMD EPYC Genoa processor. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a dense 32.0 GB memory allocation and fast NVMe storage at a lower entry point typical of shared-resource environments. This configuration is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core CPU performance, such as caching layers, memory-resident databases, and moderate-scale web applications.
description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is a memory-optimized virtual server from the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 4 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor with a shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and a 200 GB NVMe SSD, but lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and strong memory write bandwidth, though multi-core scaling and database operations perform in the lower tiers. This resource profile offers high memory density and fast local storage qualitatively suited for memory-bound workloads. It is best utilized for caching systems, memory-resident applications, and general-purpose workloads requiring high RAM-to-CPU ratios without requiring dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-4c-32gb-200s-amd is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory performance.

tagline: An AMD EPYC Genoa virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
